title: COX-2 selectivity (celecoxib vs ibuprofen) — 3LN1, 50 ns MD

C1.note: PDB 3LN1 fetched (raw_pdbs/3LN1.pdb); chain A COX-2 monomer + celecoxib (CEL) prepared. tLEaP build.log: 'Exiting LEaP: Errors = 0; Warnings = 18; Notes = 1', 5 disulfides (CYX) confirmed in prmtop, celecoxib net charge 0 (antechamber gaff2/abcg2, parmchk2 no missing params). Final system 65,490 atoms, 14,146 OPC waters, trunc-oct 10 A buffer. Residues 18-569 all present, ACE/NME caps, disordered C-tail not modeled (justified). Heme/glycan/BOG removal justified in PLAN. Topology sane.

C2.note: PLAN.md gives full tier-protocol justification with no hardcoded defaults: ff19SB (Tier2 manual p34), OPC water (Tier2 p34/p53), GAFF2/ABCG2 ligand (Tier2 p311 recommended pairing), 10 A oct box, 2 Na+ neutralization, protonation via propka3 at pH 7.4 with documented His75/GLU325 overrides. Literature precedent cited (Step 2b PMIDs). PLAN.md has explicit APPROVED gate line. RAG-first methodology evident.

C3.note: Full protocol completed: min1, min2, heat, equil1, equil2 all show Final Performance/timing blocks; prod.mdout reached NSTEP=25,000,000 (50 ns, dt=0.002). Production AVERAGES: T=299.99 K, density=1.0513 g/cc, Etot=-159,065 kcal/mol, no NaN (grep count 0). Protein RMSD ~1.2 A, ligand RMSD ~0.95 A stable to last frame. Trajectory stable, no blow-up.

C4.note: Analysis directly targets the prompt: ligand/protein RMSD (rmsd_ligand.dat ~0.9 A, rmsd_protein.dat ~1.2 A), RMSF, cpptraj hbond protein-celecoxib occupancies (hb_clean_avg.dat), sulfonamide-specific distances (dist_sulfonamide.dat), and side-pocket seating distance to Val509(=Val523) (dist_val509_sidepocket.dat ~6.1 A stable) plus ligand-pocket COM. Correct methods and observables for the binding-interaction / sulfonamide-orientation / side-pocket question.

C5.note: Paper PMID 8967954 (Kurumbail 1996, Nature) confirms the answer-key mechanism: COX-2 selectivity arises from the side pocket (Val523 in COX-2 vs Ile523 in COX-1) into which the sulfonamide of SC-558/celecoxib inserts and H-bonds (His90/Arg513/Gln192). Answer key Expected: stable in active site; sulfonamide H-bonds to Arg513, Phe518; CF3 in hydrophobic channel; side-pocket access confirmed. Agent reproduces: stable binding (ligand RMSD ~0.9 A), sulfonamide engages side pocket via Gln192 (87%) + Leu352 backbone (94%) and Phe518 backbone N-H to sulfonyl O2 (41%, hb_clean_avg.dat PHE_488=Phe518), and Val523 side-pocket seating ~6.1 A confirmed. Partial miss: the Arg513 (Arg483 in 3LN1 numbering, ARG_483 in data) sulfonamide H-bond is only transient (1.2% occupancy / >5 A), and CF3-channel placement not strongly characterized. Core expected outcome (stable + side-pocket access + Phe518 sulfonamide contact) reproduced; Arg513 deviation is physically reasonable and explicitly discussed, so high but not full credit.
